title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
JavaScript has three ways to declare variables:

• var — function-scoped, hoisted, can be re-declared. Avoid in modern JS.
• let — block-scoped, not hoisted to a usable state, cannot be re-declared in the same scope.
• const — block-scoped, must be assigned at declaration, the binding cannot be reassigned (but objects/arrays it points to are still mutable).

Rule of thumb: always use const by default; switch to let only when you need to reassign; never use var.
